title: Introducing Faster R-CNN
concept: Faster R-CNN is a fully end-to-end deep learning framework that replaces Selective Search with a Region Proposal Network (RPN).

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why was Fast R-CNN not fully end-to-end?

options.text: It relied on the external CPU-based Selective Search algorithm

exercises.explain: The CPU Selective Search step was outside the neural network, preventing end-to-end GPU execution.

cards.title: The Speed Upgrade
cards.body: If you have an automated factory, you don't hire a human clerk to hand-carry parts between conveyor belts. You build an automated mechanical arm to move the parts. In Faster R-CNN, we replace the CPU proposal step with an internal neural network on the GPU.

cards.title: Faster R-CNN
cards.body: Faster R-CNN is a landmark object detection architecture. It is fully end-to-end, replacing CPU Selective Search with a GPU-accelerated Region Proposal Network (RPN) that shares convolutional features with the detection network.

cards.title: The Automated Scout
cards.body: Instead of hiring an external scout (Selective Search) to find objects, the network trains its own internal scout (RPN) using the same brain (features). The scout works on the GPU, generating proposals in milliseconds.

cards.title: Real-time Detection
cards.body: Faster R-CNN runs at 5 to 17 frames per second (fps) on a GPU, compared to Fast R-CNN's 0.5 fps. It is the first two-stage detector capable of running close to real-time.

cards.title: Proposals still exist
cards.body: It's a myth that Faster R-CNN does away with region proposals. The proposals are still there; they are just predicted by the RPN instead of being calculated by hand-crafted pixel rules.

exercises.prompt: What major component was introduced in Faster R-CNN to replace Selective Search?
exercises.explain: Faster R-CNN introduced the Region Proposal Network (RPN) to predict candidate boxes directly on the GPU.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Faster R-CNN requires running Selective Search on the CPU before the image reaches the GPU.
exercises.explain: False. Faster R-CNN completely removes Selective Search, generating proposals internally via the RPN.
By running all modules (backbone, RPN, classifier) on the GPU, Faster R-CNN achieves high speeds.
exercises.sentence: Faster R-CNN is fully end-to-end because all stages run on the ___.

exercises.prompt: Which latency figure represents a typical Faster R-CNN system speed?
exercises.explain: Faster R-CNN runs in about 0.2 seconds (or less on modern hardware), allowing near real-time rates.

options.text: 0.2 seconds per image (~5 frames per second)

exercises.prompt: You are building a real-time object detection pipeline. Why is a fully end-to-end GPU pipeline (like Faster R-CNN) more robust than a hybrid CPU-GPU pipeline (like Fast R-CNN)?

options.text: Running everything on the GPU avoids data transfer bottlenecks between CPU and GPU, and allows the proposal network to learn from training data.
CPUs cannot run floating point math.
The GPU automatically increases the resolution of all images.
It isn't; CPU-GPU pipelines are always faster.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Eliminating CPU-GPU latency bottlenecks and enabling the RPN to learn from data is the key to both speed and accuracy. Next: let's study the inner workings of the Region Proposal Network!
